sensitivity: 0.829 (Nodule, high-sensitivity point); 0.793 (Mediastinum/Hila, high-sensitivity point); 0.853 (Consolidation, high-sensitivity point); 0.892 (Pleural Space, high-sensitivity point)
specificity: 0.956 (Nodule, high-sensitivity point); 0.975 (Mediastinum/Hila, high-sensitivity point); 0.946 (Consolidation, high-sensitivity point); 0.965 (Pleural Space, high-sensitivity point)
intended_use: ChestView US is a radiological Computer-Assisted Detection (CADe) software device that analyzes frontal and lateral chest radiographs of patients presenting with symptoms (e.g. dyspnea, cough, pain) o
intended_use_full: ChestView US is a radiological Computer-Assisted Detection (CADe) software device that analyzes frontal and lateral chest radiographs of patients presenting with symptoms (e.g. dyspnea, cough, pain) or suspected for findings related to regions of interest (ROIs) in the lungs, airways, mediastinum/hila and pleural space. The device uses machine learning techniques to identify and produces boxes around the ROIs. The boxes are labeled with one of the following radiographic findings: Nodule, Pleural space abnormality, Mediastinum/Hila abnormality, and Consolidation. ChestView US is intended for use as a concurrent reading aid for radiologists and emergency medicine physicians. It does not replace the role of radiologists and emergency medicine physicians or of other diagnostic testing in the standard of care. ChestView US is for prescription use only and is indicated for adults only.
target_condition: Regions of interest in lungs, airways, mediastinum/hila, and pleural space (nodules, consolidations, pleural effusions, pneumothorax, mediastinal/hilar enlargement)
ground_truth: Panel of U.S. board-certified radiologists who assessed the presence or absence of ROI from the indications for use of ChestView US
other_metrics: Aided versus Unaided AUC differences: Nodule 0.136 (emergency medicine) and 0.038 (radiologists); Mediastinum/Hila 0.158 (emergency medicine) and 0.057 (radiologists); Consolidation 0.099 (emergency medicine) and 0.059 (radiologists); Pleural Space 0.127 (emergency medicine) and 0.034 (radiologists). All p-values < 0.001.
